cards.text:
Bharat: Land of Wisdom and Heroes
Bharat is globally recognized for its wise and courageous individuals. The country's rich culture attracts travelers worldwide.

Cultural Richness of Bharat
The diverse culture of Bharat exemplifies unity in diversity, creating a society where people live in harmony.

Natural Abundance in Bharat
Bharat's landscapes include rivers, lakes, high mountains, and dense forests, which support diverse flora and fauna.

Prospering Bharat
Natural resources and cultural wealth contribute to Bharat's prosperity and respected status in the global community.

Role of Citizens in Bharat's Growth
Inhabitants are encouraged to work diligently to ensure Bharat's continued progress and global respect.

Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat Initiative
This program emphasizes the unity and collective identity of one nation shared among its diverse people.

Aipan: Uttarakhand Folk Art
  • A traditional art form drawn with white rice flour on red brick walls for family events and festivals.
  • Beautiful designs based on nature and cultural traditions, made by women in the family.

Dhokra Metal Craft of Odisha
  • An ancient 4000-year-old art form using clay figures, wax, and brass.
  • Uses intricate techniques of drying, waxing, and carving.
  • Involves a process where melted brass takes the shape of wax carvings.

Coconut Shell Craft in Kerala
  • Involves cleaning and smoothing coconut shells to create crafts.
  • Products include bowls and jewellery, polished for shine.
  • A sustainable, biodegradable art form.

Kondapalli Toys of Andhra Pradesh
  • A 400-year-old art form known for crafting wooden toys.
  • Items are carved individually, joined with makku paste, and painted with dyes.
  • Inspired by folk stories and rural life, depicting animals, birds, and carts.

Unity in Diversity
Unity is highlighted through shared cultural practices and art forms across different regions of Bharat.

Aakansha from Uttarakhand
Introduced Aipan folk art, drawn on special occasions, representing cultural legacy and traditional values.

Priyaranjan from Odisha
Described the detailed and delicate process of Dhokra metal craft involving clay models and brass.

Chitra from Kerala
Spoke about coconut shell craft, emphasizing creativity and eco-friendliness in everyday items.

Balamurali from Andhra Pradesh
Narrated the intricate process of creating Kondapalli wooden toys that mirror cultural narratives and life.
